scenario: Echo Banking runs Camunda 8.8 for an internal back-office platform with 2000 transactions per day. A new operator preparing onboarding notes wants a single-sentence summary of which page in Operate is used to start the documented Delete process definition flow and which kind of filter is applied on that page first.
question: Which page in Operate and which filter combination does the Delete process definition flow use as its starting point?

options.text: The Decisions page, filtered by decision name and DMN version, where the Delete control then appears at the top right of the filtered detail view of the chosen definition
The Dashboard page, filtered by Operations type and time range, where the Delete control then appears in the Operations panel for any deployment created by the current user
The Processes page, filtered by process name and version, where the Delete button at the top right then opens the confirmation dialog for the selected process definition version
The Console cluster detail page, filtered by deployment id, where the Delete control routes the request through the Administration API to remove the process definition from the cluster

a.text: Incorrect. The Decisions page and DMN version filter belong to the decision-definition delete flow, not the process-definition delete flow described in Delete process definition from Processes page.

b.text: Incorrect. The Dashboard page is not the starting point; the userguide opens this flow on the Processes page with a process name and version filter.

c.text: Correct. Step 1 of the Processes-page flow tells the operator to open the Processes page and select a specific process version by filtering by process name and version; the Delete button at the top right then opens the confirmation dialog.

d.text: Incorrect. The delete flow is described inside Operate on the Processes page, not via a Console cluster detail page or the Administration API as the starting point.

explanation: Delete process definition from Processes page starts on the Operate Processes page, where the operator filters by process name and version to select the specific definition version. The Delete button at the top right then opens the confirmation dialog before the deletion is executed.
